links.source_location: Both are agentic LLM orchestration systems with async runners, retry logic, and config-driven model selection
Cost tracking at model level — both concern cost-efficiency tradeoffs across providers
Key architectural properties: Persistent feedback loop
Key architectural properties: Single scan entrypoint
Key architectural properties: Health check endpoint
Self-improvement loop design: future LLM review pass step
Patterns applicable elsewhere — both are production engineering hygiene patterns
Core thesis: pattern is Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki
Kaggle + ARC-AGI ingest entries: Pages updated: wiki/overview.md
Every ingest entry: Pages updated: wiki/index.md
Kaggle ingest entry: Pages created: wiki/techniques/mbr-decoding.md

Context section: 'Agentic Drift' introduced as concept
Google Tunix Hackathon also uses chain-of-thought/PRM domain (per index)
graphify section: 'graph and wiki together are more powerful than either alone'
Core thesis: 'unlike RAG, which rediscovers everything from scratch'
Context: Deep Past used MBR Decoding for translation consensus
Context: single-pass LLM review is the vulnerability enabling agentic drift
Rationale: Provider Adapter enables cross-examination of models
Agentic drift is the core risk addressed in memory retention open-question
Both are visualization/navigation layers over the wiki vault
Session synthesis: 'retain-outcomes/rotate-reasoning principle applies directly to this wiki'
Cameron's approach section (contrasted with XGBoost/LightGBM stack)
Both are tabular supervised learning competitions with gradient boosting approaches
Domain context — temporal CNN/transformer over keypoint sequences
Both involve animal behavior / wildlife CV with non-standard domains; contrastive pretraining mentioned in both
Both are computer vision competitions using CNN/pretrained backbone architectures
Domain context — CLIP-style vision encoders as backbone
Domain context — explicit wikilink to sofascope.md
